class.name: RiveFileResource
class.brief_description: A Rive [code].riv[/code] file, loaded as a Godot resource.
class.description: Holds the raw bytes of a [code].riv[/code] file plus parsed metadata (artboards, state machines, inputs, view-model properties). Files load directly — [code]load("res://ui/menu.riv")[/code] — with no import step; decoding against the GPU happens lazily when an instance is created. Assign to [member RiveSprite2D.file], [member RiveControl.file], or [member RiveTexture.file] to play it.

method.name: is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the data parsed as a valid Rive file. See [method get_import_error] when it did not.

method.name: get_import_error
method.description: Human-readable reason the file failed to parse (empty when valid).

method.name: get_artboard_names
method.description: Names of every artboard in the file.

method.name: get_state_machine_names
method.description: State machine names of [param artboard] (empty string = default artboard).

method.name: get_input_descriptions
method.description: State machine inputs as Dictionaries: [code]{ name, type: "bool"|"number"|"trigger", default }[/code]. Empty strings select the defaults.

method.name: get_property_descriptions
method.description: View-model properties of the artboard's default view model as Dictionaries: [code]{ name, type, enum_values? }[/code].

method.name: get_artboard_size
method.description: Authored artboard size in Rive units.

method.name: get_asset_descriptions
method.description: Assets the file references, embedded or not: an [Array] of [Dictionary] with [code]name[/code], [code]unique_name[/code], [code]unique_filename[/code], [code]type[/code] ("image"/"font"/"audio"/"other"), and [code]resolved[/code]. Unresolved entries are out-of-band: place the asset next to the [code].riv[/code] using Rive's export naming ([code]name-id.ext[/code]) and it resolves automatically.

method.name: add_fallback_font
method.description: Registers a global fallback font (a [FontFile] or raw TTF/OTF [PackedByteArray]) consulted, in registration order, whenever an authored font is missing a glyph (CJK, localization, symbols). Returns [code]false[/code] if the data fails to decode. Register before loading text-bearing scenes.
